finding_results.notes: Replaced three theme-mouthpiece lines with concrete trader-register speech. (1) 'You don't know the sound' replaced with 'I've been picking her up off the kitchen floor since spring' — specific, physical, no balanced antithesis. (2) 'My girl's my zero' replaced with 'I've still got a healer in Roth waiting on the second half of a bill / That's the road I should be on' — trader vocabulary (bills, roads) instead of Vel's threshold language. (3) 'once a road starts needing this much not-talking, somebody's paying for it besides you' replaced with 'the kid still doesn't know why he's walking south. I can see that from here. I'd rather not be around when he works it out' — direct accusation, not a quotable aphorism. Also removed narrator commentary at former line 65 ('That landed harder than if he'd raised his voice') which admired Dren's dialogue. Updated the end-of-chapter callback at line 710 to match the new concrete line.

Added two visible moments of self-correction to Vel's confession. (1) At line 279, Vel starts with the technical framing ('The cascade needed someone who could—'), registers that Kem is watching and waiting for the real sentence, and restarts with the uglier truth ('I went looking for somebody to use'). Kem's visible reaction (line 281: 'something behind his face shifted') follows the corrected disclosure. (2) At line 337, Vel begins the drowning-girl story as self-justification ('because a girl was drowning and the Hoarded standing on the bank wouldn't—'), looks at Kem, sees his face has gone still, and names her own dodge ('That's the version that makes me sound like I've always been the person who spends for strangers. Maren was... I went looking for someone to spend. That's different.'). Both friction points show competing impulses disrupting the confession's surface. Kem's watching at the cascade transition (line 279) directly influences Vel's shift from the safe framing to the honest one.

summary: Revised Dren's departure speech to eliminate three theme-mouthpiece lines (balanced antithesis, thematic equivalence, quotable aphorism) and one instance of narrator commentary admiring dialogue, replacing all with concrete trader-register speech grounded in Mira's medical costs, physical caregiving, and direct accusation about Kem's ignorance. Added two moments of visible self-correction to Vel's confession: a false start on the technical/safe framing of the cascade mechanism that Kem's watching forces into honest disclosure, and a mid-sentence catch of the drowning-girl story being deployed as mitigation. Both friction points show competing impulses (truth-telling vs. self-interest) disrupting the confession's surface in ways consistent with Vel's decisive self-correction style.
